sections.content:

It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Black F merged with bottom border at [54,0]-[63,62], charcoal F at [54,0]-[62,8], yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59]. DOWN 15 caused black F to move to bottom and merge with border.
- Goal model: Need to get black F to overlap with yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59].
- Plan: ACTION7 to separate black F, then UP 9 to overlap with yellow F.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 315 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 9.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image:
- Charcoal F is at bottom left
- Black F is just below the yellow F on the right
- Yellow F is at [45,51]-[53,59]
The black F is now separated from the bottom border. It's at approximately [54,54]-[62,62]. The yellow F is at [45,51]-[53,59].
To overlap the black F with the yellow F, I need to move UP 9 (from row 54 to row 45) and LEFT 3 (from col 54 to col 51).
But wait, the columns don't match exactly. The black F is at cols 54-62 and the yellow F is at cols 51-59. If I move LEFT 3, the black F would be at cols 51-59, matching the yellow F's columns.
So the plan is: UP 9 + LEFT 3.
But I need to be careful about the game over condition. The black F is on the right and the charcoal F is on the left. If they're at the same row range, would they swap and cause game over?
From my previous experience, the game over happened when the black F and charcoal F were at the same row range on opposite sides. But in this case, the black F is at rows 54-62 and the charcoal F is at rows 54-62. They're at the same row range!
Wait, but the image shows the charcoal F at the bottom left and the black F at the bottom right. They're at the same row range (54-62) but on opposite sides. If the game over condition is triggered, the game would be over already.
But the game is not over. So maybe the game over condition is not triggered when they're at the same row range at the bottom.
Let me just try UP 9 + LEFT 3 and see what happens.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the black F is at the bottom right, just below the yellow F. The charcoal F is at the bottom left. They're at the same row range (54-62).
If I move UP 9, the black F would move to rows 45-53, overlapping with the yellow F. The charcoal F would also move UP 9, to rows 45-53.
After UP 9:
- Black F: [45,54]-[53,62]
- Charcoal F: [45,0]-[53,8]
- Yellow F: [45,51]-[53,59]
The black F and yellow F would partially overlap (black F at cols 54-62, yellow F at cols 51-59). The overlap would be at cols 54-59.
Then LEFT 3 would move the black F to cols 51-59, fully overlapping with the yellow F.
But the charcoal F would also move LEFT 3, to cols -3 to 5. Since cols can't be negative, the charcoal F would be at cols 0-5 or something.
Let me try UP 9 + LEFT 3.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Black F at [54,54]-[62,62] (bottom right, below yellow F), charcoal F at [54,0]-[62,8] (bottom left), yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59]. ACTION7 separated black F from border.
Goal model: Move black F to overlap with yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59].
Plan: UP 9 + LEFT 3 to align black F with yellow F.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# UP 9 + LEFT 3 to overlap black F with yellow F
action(['UP']*9 + ['LEFT']*3)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
